LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT THE STANDARD FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. NEITHER JURY'S FINDING OF MALICE FOR PURPOSES OF CIVIL CODE SECTION 3294 NOR GUILTY PLEAS TO GENERAL INTENT MISDEMEANORS NOR PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGATIONS IN THE UNDERLYING COMPLAINT ESTABLISHES AS A MATTER OF LAW THAT INSURANCE CODE 533 BARS COVERAGE. SEE PETERSON VS. SUPERIOR COURT 1982 31 CAL. 3D 147. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS LIKEWISE DENIED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR # 8189 =PJB/CK

LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF GERRY ROBERTSON'S MOTION TO AMEND SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED ON THE FOLLOWING CONDITIONS: 1) THE PLAINTIFF MUST COOPERATE WITH ALL REASONABLE DISCOVERY ON THE NEW ALLEGATIONS TO THE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT; 2) THE PLAINTIFF MUST NOT OBJECT TO A TRIAL CONTINUANCE; 3) PLAINTIFF MUST STIPULATE TO SHORTEN TIME WITH RESPECT TO A MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. LAW AND MOTION 301, ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF GERRY ROBERTSON'S MOTION TO AMEND SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. PROPOSED AMENDMENT WOULD SUBSTANTIALLY ENLARGE THE ISSUES IN THE CASE AND IMPROPERLY INJECTS CLAIMS THAT AROSE LONG AFTER THE COMPLAINT WAS FILED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT BEEN DILIGENT IN SEEKING LEAVE TO AMEND. DEFENDANT WOULD BE PREJUDICED GIVEN THAT DISCOVERY HAS CLOSED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR 8189

Philip T. Prince of the Law Offices of Philip T. Prince, 981-7322, appearing for plaintiff and moving party
Renee A. Haupt of Lindahl Beck, LLP, 213-488-3900, appearing via Court Call for defendant and responding party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF GERRY ROBERTSON'S Motion To Amend Second Amended Complaint IS DENIED. PROPOSED AMENDMENT WOULD SUBSTANTIALLY ENLARGE THE ISSUES IN THE CASE AND IMPROPERLY INJECTS CLAIMS THAT AROSE LONG AFTER THE COMPLAINT WAS FILED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT BEEN DILIGENT IN SEEKING LEAVE TO AMEND. DEFENDANT WOULD BE PREJUDICED GIVEN THAT DISCOVERY HAS CLOSED. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (301/PJB/CK) Judge: Peter J. Off Calendar May-10-2007 Continued To Jan-28-08 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motioon For Protective Order Re: Current Litigation Materials;
Off Calendar May-22-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Renee A. Haupt, Esq. for Defendant & moving party: The Standard Fire Ins.
Demian I. Oksenendler/Philp T. Prince, Esq., for plaintiff & moving/opposing party: Gerry K. Robertson.
Cheryl Wilder, CSR

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant The Standard Fire Insurance Company's Motion For Protective Order re: Current Litigation Materials is granted as it seeks to bar discovery relating to defendant's conduct, defense, strategy, or knowledge regarding the present action.
The issue is not whether actions occurring after the commencement of the litigation must be raised in a supplemental complaint. The issue is that the bad faith must be raised by the actions of counsel and the party, not by the strategy or work product produced in the course of litigation. Work product and confidential communications falling within recognizable privilege remain privileged. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-02-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES- ATTY. E. GERARD MANNION OF MANNION & LOWE, TEL. NO. 733-1052 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. RENEE A. HAUPT OF LINDAHL & BECK, TEL. NO. (213)488-3910 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT STANDARD FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY.

RULING - A R G U E D. THE COURT ADOPTED IT'S TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS - DEFENDANT THE STANDARD FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. NEITHER JURY'S FINDING OF MALICE FOR PURPOSES OF CIVIL CODE SECTION 3294 NOR GUILTY PLEAS TO GENERAL INTENT MISDEMEANORS NOR PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGATIONS IN THE UNDERLYING COMPLAINT ESTABLISHES AS A MATTER OF LAW THAT INSURANCE CODE 533 BARS COVERAGE. SEE PETERSON VS. SUPERIOR COURT 1982 31 CAL. 3D 147. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS LIKEWISE DENIED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =PJB/CK
